question: Is the general classroom environment (tiered lecture hall, wooden seats, windows) rendered in a photorealistic style with daylight streaming in?
Is the professor at the podium rendered in a Cubist style, featuring geometrically fragmented planes and multi-perspective facial features?
Is the student in the first row rendered in Japanese cel animation style, exhibiting crisp outlines and flat cel-shaded shadows?
Is the student in the second row rendered in Renaissance fresco style, showing plaster texture and faded pigments?
Is the third student rendered in paper collage/cut-out style, clearly showing layered torn-edge paper fragments?
Is the teaching assistant rendered in a photorealistic style with visible motion blur?
Does the projection screen display isometric technical line art or blueprint style graphics (picture-within-a-picture)?
Is the professor positioned at the podium, turning around and explaining with hand gestures?
Is the cel animation style student in the first row raising their hand?
Is the Renaissance fresco style student in the second row showing head down taking notes?
Does the paper collage-style student partially occlude the Renaissance fresco-style student?
Do all subjects, despite their varying styles, share a unified perspective consistent with the classroom geometry?
Do all subjects share a unified lighting direction and cast shadows consistent with the classroom environment?
Does the content on the projection screen consist only of geometric shapes without any text?
Is the image free of readable text on the screen, notebooks, or elsewhere?
